title: Absence of Integrity Controls on Personal Data
description: Storing or transmitting personal data without integrity verification (HMAC, digital signature, or authenticated encryption) exposes it to undetected tampering, violating GDPR Article 32(1)(b) (integrity and confidentiality).

finding.message: Personal data stored/transmitted without apparent integrity control at '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Use authenticated encryption (AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305) which provides both confidentiality and integrity.
Add an HMAC-SHA256 to serialized personal data payloads before storage or transmission.
GDPR Article 32(1)(b) requires 'the ongoing integrity and confidentiality of processing systems'.
For database records, consider row-level checksums or application-level signing.
